steps.title: Write the line equation
steps.content:
The line passes through $(-20,0)$ and has slope $-\frac{3}{4}$. Use point-slope form:
$$y-0=-\frac{3}{4}(x-(-20))$$
$$y=-\frac{3}{4}(x+20)$$

steps.title: Read the intercept
steps.content:
Expand to slope-intercept form:
$$y=-\frac{3}{4}x-\frac{3}{4}\cdot20$$
$$y=-\frac{3}{4}x-15$$
The constant term is the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept. The answer is -15.
